roadmapItems.title: Introduction to Pressure and Fluids
roadmapItems.summary: Establishes foundational understanding of pressure, fluid behavior, and atmospheric effects. Prepares the learner for deeper exploration of fluid dynamics and gas laws.

questions.question_text: Atmospheric pressure is due to the weight
options: density and volume of the atmosphere

questions.question_text: A barometer that uses water instead of mercury will be
When wind speeds up as it blows over the top of a hill, atmospheric pressure there

roadmapItems.title: Buoyancy and Fluid Displacement
roadmapItems.summary: Explores Archimedes' principle and buoyant forces in fluids. Builds conceptual foundation for understanding floating, sinking, and displacement phenomena.

questions.question_text: A completely submerged object always displaces its own
An inflated balloon with a heavy rock tied to it is dropped into a deep lake. As the balloon sinks deeper and deeper the buoyant force acting on it
The flight of a blimp best illustrates

roadmapItems.title: Gas Laws and Ideal Gas Behavior
roadmapItems.summary: Focuses on ideal gas law, pressure-volume-temperature relationships, and real-world corrections. Develops quantitative reasoning for gas systems.

questions.question_text: What happens to the pressure, P, of an ideal gas if the temperature is increased by a factor of 2 and the volume is increased by a factor of 8 ?
options: P decreases by a factor of 16.
P decreases by a factor of 4.
P decreases by a factor of 2.
P increases by a factor of 4.

questions.question_text: When using the ideal gas law, standard conditions for temperature and pressure are
The ideal gas law is successful for most gases because
options: gas particles do not interact significantly

questions.question_text: If a gas occupies 0.1L at 200atm, what will its volume be at 1atm?
When collecting a gas over water, it is important to
options: be sure the gas does not burn
correct for the vapor pressure of water
questions.topic: partial pressure and vapor pressure correction

questions.question_text: There are a number of experiments for the determination of the molecular mass of a gas. One method begins by heating a solid or liquid in a flask to produce a gaseous product. The gas passes through a...
options: The heat of formation of the gas
The mass of the displaced water
The vapor pressure of the water

roadmapItems.title: Fluid Dynamics and Bernoulli's Principle
roadmapItems.summary: Introduces streamline flow, pressure variation in moving fluids, and Bernoulli's principle. Connects theory to real-world phenomena like lift and flow restriction.

questions.question_text: A fluid of density ρ flows through a horizontal pipe with negligible viscosity. The flow is streamlined with constant flow rate. The diameter of the pipe at Point 1 is d and the flow speed is V. If th...
options: less than the pressure at Point 1 by 4 ρv^2
less than the pressure at Point 1 by 40 ρv^2
more than the pressure at Point 1 by 4 ρv^2
more than the pressure at Point 1 by 40 ρv^2
questions.topic: Bernoulli's equation and pressure in constricted pipes

questions.question_text: What is the work involved if a gas in a 2.0-liter container at 2.4 atmospheres pressure is allowed to expand against a pressure of 0.80 atmosphere?

roadmapItems.title: Advanced Concepts in Fluids and Phase Behavior
roadmapItems.summary: Covers phase changes, effusion, and intermolecular forces in fluid contexts. Reinforces conceptual depth and prepares learner for advanced applications.

questions.question_text: When a gas turns into a liquid, the process is called
Helium effuses through a pinhole 5.33 times faster than an unknown gas. That gas is most likely
What is the only metal that is a liquid at normal atmospheric pressure and temperature?
